content.cn_text: 吉姆:简，你觉得摩根教授的课怎么样?我不知道你是怎么想的，但我觉得很难相信光会像他说的那样影响环境。我从未见过任何期刊文章、网站或任何能证实他的观点的东西。它是愚蠢的。
content.en_text: Jim: Jane, what did you think of Professor Morgan's lecture? I don't know about you, but I find it incredibly difficult to believe that light influences the environment as much as he says. I've never seen any journal articles, websites or anything that verifies his argument.It's stupid.

content.cn_text: 简:恰恰相反，我从众多著名科学家那里看到了大量支持他观点的研究。你看过学期初给我们的课程大纲上推荐的课本了吗?所有的信息都在里面，也许你只是找错地方了。
content.en_text: Jane: On the contrary, I've seen a great deal of research supporting his argument from a wide range of renowned scientists. Have you looked at the recommended textbook listed on the course outline given to us at the beginning of the semester? All the information is in there, perhaps you've just been looking in the wrong places.

content.cn_text: 吉姆:我从来不看课程大纲，我有很多散乱的纸，以至于在一天结束的时候，我往往会把交给我的东西弄丢。他们推荐的教科书是什么?我从哪里可以买到?我应该马上去买;我的功课已经落后了。
content.en_text: Jim: I never look at the course outlines, I have so many loose sheets of paper I tend to lose anything I'm given by the end of the day. What's the textbook they recommend, and where can I get it from? I should probably go buy it soon; I'm already behind in the course.

content.cn_text: 简:是啊，你绝对应该买，而且今年我们的成绩更重要!它被称为“光对环境的影响”。你应该能在校园里的书店里找到这本书。如果没有，他们会在两周内订购。与此同时，你应该读一读肯·辛普森的著作，他认为为了保护自然栖息地，政府应该努力在晚上关掉城市的灯。
content.en_text: Jane: Yeah, you definitely should buy it, and our grades are more important this year! It's called 'The Influence of Light on the Environment'. You should be able to find it in the bookshop on campus. If not, they'll order it within two weeks. In the meantime, you should read up on Ken Simpson's work, he argues that in order to protect natural habitats,governments should endeavor to turn off lights in cities at night.

content.cn_text: 吉姆:这是有争议的;我怀疑任何政府都不愿意在短期内这么做。我想如果没有路灯，道路会变得非常危险。对于这个问题，戴夫·开普勒建议他们可以用更环保的灯泡取代现有的灯。他们甚至可以安装太阳能灯;这样，道路将更加环保，同时保持安全。虽然我想它们在寒冷的国家不会特别有效，尤其是在冬天。
content.en_text: Jim: Well that's controversial; I doubt any government would be willing to do that any time soon. I imagine roads would become quite dangerous without street lighting. For this issue, Dave Kepler suggests they could just replace the existing lights with more environmentally friendly bulbs. They could even install solar-powered lights; that way,roads will be more eco-friendly while maintaining safety. Although I guess they wouldn't be particularly effective in colder countries, especially during the winter.

content.cn_text: 简:这真是个好主意。预计在未来几十年内，太阳能的价格将与电力持平，今天早上的新闻也报道了这一消息。我还听说，根据莎伦·格雷的说法，在阳光充足的国家，以昆虫为食的动物往往体型更小。因为昆虫少了，剩下的昆虫产的卵也少了。
content.en_text: Jane: That's quite a good idea actually. The price of solar power is supposed to be on par with electricity within the next few decades, and it was on the news this morning. I've also heard that, according to Sharon Grey, in countries with more sunlight,insect-eating animals tend to be smaller in size. Since there are fewer insects, and the remaining insects produced a smaller number of eggs.

content.cn_text: 吉姆:对，我想我在什么地方读到过阳光对水质也有负面影响，不过我不确定我是否相信。在许多炎热的国家，特别是发展中国家，有很多水污染是由工厂而不是阳光造成的。
content.en_text: Jim: Yeah, I think I read somewhere that sunlight also has a negative effect on the quality of water, but I'm not sure I believe it. In many hot countries, particularly developing countries, there is a lot of water pollution caused by factories rather than sunlight.

content.cn_text: 然而，玛丽亚·杰克逊说，在阳光直射下，水面变得更加半透明，因此它影响了水生昆虫可以吸收的阳光量。没有太多的研究来证明杰克逊的理论，但它似乎已经被广泛接受了。
content.en_text: Nevertheless, Maria Jackson says that in direct sunlight, the surface of the water becomes more translucent, therefore it affects the amount of sunlight that aquatic insects can absorb. Not much research has been undertaken to prove Jackson's theory, but it seems to have been widely accepted anyway.

content.cn_text: 简:我从来没听说过。我得在谷歌上查一下。我研究过的另一个理论是芭芭拉·斯沃洛关于昆虫数量减少如何对青蛙数量产生不利影响的研究。我不是在抱怨，我讨厌昆虫，尤其是蜘蛛。
content.en_text: Jane: I've never heard of that. I'll have to look it up on Google. The only other theory I've studied is Barbara Swallow's study on how declined insect population adversely affects the frog population. Not that I'm complaining.. .1 hate insects, especially spiders.

content.cn_text: 吉姆:你有蜘蛛恐惧症?我怎么也想不到。你哥哥不是养过一只黑寡妇蜘蛛吗?
content.en_text: Jim: You have arachnophobia? I never would have guessed. Didn't your brother have a pet black widow spider?

content.cn_text: 简:是的，他这样做了，我讨厌这样。有一次它从笼子里逃出来，我们再也没找到它。我做了好几个月的噩梦。
content.en_text: Jane: Yes, he did, and I hated it. It escaped from its cage once and we never found it. I had nightmares for months.

content.cn_text: 简:好吧，现在我都起鸡皮疙瘩了，我们换个话题吧。你对自然光和人造光的看法是什么?
content.en_text: Jane: Okay, now I'm getting goose bumps, let's change the subject. What's your stance on natural and artificial light?

content.cn_text: 吉姆:老实说，我不确定你用哪种有多大区别;无论哪种方式，物种都会灭绝。我认为我们真正应该考虑的是全球变暖和保护或替代有限的燃料。太阳能为我们提供了一个难以置信的机会来取代电力，政府绝对应该增加在这一领域的研究支出。 .我们课上讨论的理论，比如辛普森和格蕾的理论，都很模糊，缺乏证据，所以我不明白我们为什么还要学习它们。
content.en_text: Jim: Honestly, I'm not sure it makes much difference which one you use; species will die out either way. I think the real argument we should consider is global warming and protection or replacement of finite fuels. Solar power provides us with an incredible opportunity to replace electricity, and governments should definitely increase spending on research in this field.  The theories discussed in our lectures, like Simpson's and Grey's, are so vague and lack proof, so I don't understand why we even study them.

content.cn_text: 简:我明白你的意思了。我不喜欢为了考试而学习没有根据的理论，我宁愿把时间花在学习其他东西上。例如，我更感兴趣的是研究野生动物园的动物，而不是研究候鸟，尤其是游客对动物生活质量的影响。我们知道，每年都有成千上万的游客会开着自己的车或乘坐该设施提供的车辆来观察自由漫游的动物。
content.en_text: Jane: I see what you mean. I don't like learning unsupported theories for exams, and I'd rather spend my time learning something else. For example, I'd be much more interested in studying the animals in safari parks than researching migratory birds,particularly the effect of tourists on the quality of life of animals. As we know, every year thousands of visitors will drive in their own vehicles or ride in vehicles provided by the facility to observe freely roaming animals.

content.cn_text: 吉姆:是啊，那一定很有趣!尤其是那些生活在热带国家的动物，比如婆罗洲。接着，我想研究把动物从国外带到我们的动物园是如何影响它们的预期寿命的。比如，你还记得中国送大熊猫去爱丁堡动物园吗?显然，其中一只熊猫变得抑郁了，但从来没有解释过原因。对我来说，很明显，你不能把一只动物带出它的自然栖息地，把它关在世界另一端的笼子里。就是行不通……(淡出)
content.en_text: Jim: Yeah, that would be really interesting! Especially those animals living in more tropical countries, like Borneo. Following on from that,  I want to study how bringing animals over from foreign countries to put in our zoos affects their life expectancy. For example, do you remember when China sent pandas to Edinburgh Zoo? Apparently one of the pandas became depressed, but it was never explained why. To me, obviously, you can't take an animal out of its natural habitat and put it in a cage on the other side of the world. It just doesn't work... [fade out]

groups.desc:
Questions 21-25
What is the main opinion of each of the following people?
Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-G, next to questions 21-25.
Opinions

list.title: D
list.content: decline in the number of species

list.title: F
list.content: impacts on the growth of animals

items.title: Ken Simpson
items.analysis: Ken Simpson主张政府应该在夜间关闭城市的灯光，这明显是一种限制照明的措施。"lighting restrictions"（照明限制）正是对Simpson观点的准确概括。他提出这一建议是为了保护自然栖息地，表明他认为过度照明会对环境造成负面影响。因此，A选项"lighting restrictions"最准确地反映了Ken Simpson的主要观点。

items.title: Dave Kepler
items.analysis: Dave Kepler提出了一种替代照明的方案，即用更环保的灯泡替换现有的灯，甚至安装太阳能灯。这种方法既能保持道路安全，又能提高环保性。因此，Kepler的观点明确支持了替代照明的概念，与选项B "alternative lighting"完全吻合。

items.title: Sharon Grey
items.analysis: Sharon Grey的主要观点是关于阳光对动物生长的影响。她指出，在阳光更充足的国家，食虫动物的体型往往更小。这直接对应了选项F"impacts on the growth of animals"（对动物生长的影响）。虽然原文也提到了昆虫数量的减少，但这是造成动物体型变小的原因，而不是Grey的主要观点。因此，F选项最准确地概括了Sharon Grey的核心观点。

items.title: Maria Jackson
items.analysis: Maria Jackson的主要观点集中在阳光对水质的影响上。她指出，直射阳光使水面变得更加透明，这直接影响了水质，进而影响了水生昆虫吸收阳光的能力。这明确表明她的观点与水质的变化有关，而不是其他选项提到的内容。因此，G选项"impacts on water quality"（对水质的影响）是最准确的描述。

items.title: Barbara Swallow
items.analysis: Barbara Swallow的研究主要关注昆虫数量减少对青蛙种群的负面影响。这表明她的研究重点是物种数量的下降，特别是昆虫和青蛙这两个物种之间的关系。因此，选项D "decline in the number of species"（物种数量的下降）最准确地概括了她研究的主要观点。

groups.desc:
Questions 26-27
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.title: What does Jim think about the difference among species in natural and artificial light?

options.title: B
options.content: Species will die out in natural light.

options.title: C
options.content: Species will die out in artificial light.

items.analysis: Jim明确表示他不确定自然光和人工光是否有区别，并认为无论使用哪种光，物种都会灭绝。这直接对应了选项A"It makes no difference"（没有区别）。Jim的表述"I'm not sure it makes much difference"和"species will die out either way"清楚地表明了他认为自然光和人工光对物种的影响没有区别。

items.title: One reason why Jim and Jane felt unsatisfied about the theories discussed in the lecture was that

options.title: A
options.content: governments didn't increase any spending on them.

options.title: B
options.content: most theories had nothing to do with the exams.

options.title: C
options.content: many theories lacked solid proof in the field.

items.analysis: Jim明确表示讲座中讨论的理论"vague and lack proof"（模糊且缺乏证据），这直接对应了选项C中的"lacked solid proof"（缺乏坚实的证据）。Jim的这个观点代表了他和Jane共同的不满，因为Jane之前也表示不喜欢学习未经证实的理论。因此，选项C最准确地反映了他们对讲座理论不满意的原因。

groups.title: Which THREE topics are they interested in studying in the future?

collect_options.title: Which THREE topics are they interested in studying in the future?

list.title: F
list.content: the impacts of different environment on animals

items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B, E和F，因为对话中明确表示了对这三个主题的兴趣。Jane表示她对研究野生动物园里的动物更感兴趣，这对应选项B。Jim提到了对热带国家动物的兴趣，特别是婆罗洲，这对应选项E。最后，Jim表示想研究将动物从国外引进到动物园如何影响它们的寿命，这对应选项F。这三个主题都是他们明确表示有兴趣在未来研究的。
这道题的正确答案是B, E和F，因为对话中明确表示了对这三个主题的兴趣。Jane表示她对研究野生动物园里的动物更感兴趣，这对应选项B。Jim提到了对热带国家动物的兴趣，特别是婆罗洲，这对应选项E。最后，Jim表示想研究将动物从国外引进到动物园如何影响它们的寿命，这对应选项F。这三个主题都是他们明确表示有兴趣在未来研究的。
这道题的正确答案是B, E和F，因为对话中明确表示了对这三个主题的兴趣。Jane表示她对研究野生动物园里的动物更感兴趣，这对应选项B。Jim提到了对热带国家动物的兴趣，特别是婆罗洲，这对应选项E。最后，Jim表示想研究将动物从国外引进到动物园如何影响它们的寿命，这对应选项F。这三个主题都是他们明确表示有兴趣在未来研究的。
